questions.question: What is at the core of all healthy team interaction?
What are the four stages of team building most often experienced by teams?
During which stage of team building do conflicts begin to arise as members tend to resist the influence of the team and rebel against accomplishing the task?
During the forming stage of team building, what occurs?
options: Team members develop proficiency in achieving goals
Conflicts arise as members resist the team's influence
Individuals try to determine their place on the team and establish or accept procedures and rules

questions.question: Which stage of team building is described as the point where the team has settled its relationships and expectations, and can begin diagnosing and solving problems?
A sign that a team is not performing cohesively is when what exists among team members?
What are the three sources of conflict identified in the text?
options: Communication factors, structural factors, and personal behavior factors
Leadership factors, organizational factors, and environmental factors
Financial factors, operational factors, and personnel factors
Strategic factors, tactical factors, and logistical factors

questions.question: According to Dr. Kenneth Thomas, the competing (forcing) conflict management style is characterized by what levels of assertiveness and cooperativeness?
Which conflict management style uses an attempt to satisfy the concerns of both sides through honest discussion and seeks a 'win' position for both groups?
The accommodating conflict management style is characterized by what levels of assertiveness and cooperativeness?
Warren G. Bennis labeled three primary behavioral leader characteristics as the abilities to do what?
According to Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs, what must be achieved before reaching self-actualization?
options: Only physiological needs must be met
Only esteem and safety needs must be met
Basic requirements at each previous level must be achieved
Only belongingness and love needs must be met

questions.question: Bennis labeled three primary behavioral characteristics of managers as what?
USAF mentoring is governed by which publication?
options: AFI 36-2406, Officer and Enlisted Evaluation Systems

questions.question: In the mentoring model (M-E-N-T-O-R-I-N-G), what does the 'E' stand for?
In the mentoring model, what does the 'G' at the end of M-E-N-T-O-R-I-N-G stand for?
Which counseling approach is preferred for most counseling sessions?
The directive counseling approach works best in which situation?
options: To correct simple problems, make on-the-spot corrections, and correct specific aspects of duty performance
When the Airman needs to develop personal responsibility
When there is ample time for the Airman to explore options

questions.question: What are the four basic components of a counseling session?
options: State the purpose, discuss the issues, develop a plan of action, and record the plan
Identify, analyze, resolve, and follow up

questions.question: Full Range Leadership Development (FRLD) requires leadership to be viewed as a system made up of which three core elements?
options: The leader, the follower, and the situation

questions.question: Which FRLD leadership behavior is described as an 'if it's not broke, don't fix it' style where leaders sit back, observe, and wait for things to go wrong before taking action?
What are the four components of transformational leadership?
options: Individualized consideration, intellectual stimulation, idealized influence, and inspirational motivation

questions.question: Which source of power comes from personal character attributes such as strength of character, passion, inspiration, or a personal vision of the future?
According to the chapter, which conflict management style requires a willingness of both parties to change, adjust, or give something up and seeks to partially satisfy both parties' desires?
In the norming stage of team building, what happens to competitive relationships?
options: They intensify as members compete for leadership roles
They become more cooperative as emotional conflict is reduced
They are eliminated entirely through enforced rules
They are redirected toward external competitors
